name: ETH 4h Deep Value Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: a deeper selloff into structural support reclaims value and rotates away from a failed downside auction inside a chop regime
thesis: Cycle 85 wants trend pullback continuation after deeper value reclaim into structural support, but the live regime map says ETH 4h is still the cleanest pure-chop lane. So the mechanism has to be expressed as a failed downside auction reclaim, not as naive trend following. This is denser and more structurally honest than recent sparse recovery theater.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h deep reclaim events still fail to clear moderate density or reclaimed value does not produce enough rotation after costs, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pushes below short-term value into structural support, reclaims value, and rotates upward as the failed breakdown unwinds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a pure chop regime, failed downside excursions should mean revert more often than they trend. Reclaiming value after deeper support interaction is the cleanest mechanism-first way to express that.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is the cleanest chop-owned lane in the basket and should reward failed downside auctions returning to balance more than continuation logic would.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: deep excursion threshold and risk sizing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may be only a weak bounce inside a genuine trend-down leg, causing repeated failed mean reversion.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trades appear but expectancy is thin, move profit-taking closer before changing the reclaim trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated modest balance rotations, frequent first-target captures, and a few larger unwind moves.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears moderate density and materially out-trades the prior sparse reclaim ideas while keeping losses contained.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reclaim entries that lose value immediately.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because failed downside excursions in chop should recur enough

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit chop-owned lane for deep value reclaim.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reclaim rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h reclaim entries remain too sparse or fail immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with many balance rotations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim grammar survives on the cycle primary transition lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 4h preserves a tradable failed-auction reclaim behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h transition pressure breaks the mean-reversion thesis.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly larger but less frequent reclaim rotations than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism gains density on the adjacent timeframe without collapsing into the 1h noise pattern seen before.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 1h increases count while preserving actual reclaim structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller reclaim rotations than ETH 4h.

long: Exit remaining 20% on loss of VWAP or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on reclaim of VWAP or 5 bars
